explanations.6: debugpy is the debugging protocol that Python uses; this tells VS Code to use Python's debugger instead of a generic one.
explanations.7: attach means VS Code will connect to a debugger that's already running somewhere, rather than starting a new one.
explanations.8: connect specifies where to find the running debugger—here it's on your local machine at a specific port number.
explanations.12: justMyCode set to true means the debugger will skip stepping into Python library code and only debug your own project files.
explanations.13: pathMappings tells the debugger how to translate file paths between your computer and wherever the code is actually running.
